summary: Three existing tests correctly updated and two new tests added. Each new test exercises observable behavior, not implementation. Litmus check: removing each affected production line of code (tarRecorder.write() sysinfo emission, view.go NotRecordable=false on procpidstat, filterViews() NotRecordable check, isFilenameOK sysinfo prefix, describeReport map entry for procpidstat, processData/readTar pipeline for procpidstat) causes at least one assertion to fail. Test pyramid unchanged — all updates and additions live at the unit level, consistent with existing record_test.go and report_test.go style. Two minor coverage observations (see findings); neither blocks acceptance.

findings.issue: Output assertions are weak: assert.Contains(out, "/") and assert.Contains(out, "SELECT 1"). Both substrings appear exactly once in the expected output (the timestamp line '2026/05/19 10:00:01, rate: 1s' and the query column), so the assertions are correct today. However, if a future change accidentally suppressed the data row but kept the timestamp header, the test would still pass on '/'. A tighter assertion (e.g. matching the timestamp via regex '\d{4}/\d{2}/\d{2}' and counting at least one occurrence of the pid '1234' in addition to 'SELECT 1') would catch a wider class of regressions.
findings.recommendation: Optional: add assert.Regexp(t, regexp.MustCompile(`\d{4}/\d{2}/\d{2}`), out) and assert.Contains(t, out, "1234") to pin the timestamp format and the data-row presence independently of the query column.

findings.issue: The totalViews formula now depends on countRecordable(view.New()) which is computed once at test-function entry. If a future task adds another non-recordable view, the formula keeps working; if a task adds a new per-tick non-stat tar entry (like a second sysinfo-style metadata blob), the +2 constant becomes wrong. The risk is low because the test will fail loudly (filesCount mismatch), but the +2 itself is not self-documenting — only the comment ties it to 'meta + sysinfo'.
findings.recommendation: Optional: replace the magic '+2' with named constants (e.g. metaEntriesPerTick + sysinfoEntriesPerTick) or comment that lists each non-stat entry explicitly. Not blocking — the comment already explains the +2 origin clearly.

litmusChecks.production_line: record/recorder.go: sysinfo entry written in tarRecorder.write() (line ~262)
internal/view/view.go: NotRecordable field removed from procpidstat (now false)
litmusChecks.failing_test: Test_filterViews (every row's wantN and wantV would mismatch); TestFilterViews_NotRecordable (assert.False on pp.NotRecordable)

litmusChecks.production_line: record/record.go: NotRecordable drop branch in filterViews() (line 210)
report/report.go: procpidstat entry in describeReport map (line 627)
report/report.go: sysinfo prefix in isFilenameOK; sysinfo branch in readTar; processData full pipeline for procpidstat
litmusChecks.failing_test: Test_app_doReport_procpidstat (non-empty output, timestamp line, data row)

coverage.acceptanceCriteriaCoverage: all 8 AC items from the task spec verified via test runs
